Styles › High-Tech

1970s – present · UK · Global

High-Tech

The structure and services are the architecture.

  • Overview
  • Design Philosophy
  • Visual Characteristics
  • Materials
  • Color Palette
  • Façade Guide
  • Lighting
  • Building Types
  • Design Checklist
  • Common Mistakes
  • AI Prompts
  • Gallery
  • Related

01 — Overview

Overview

Definition. High-Tech celebrates engineering as aesthetic — exposing structure, services and circulation as the building's expressive language in steel, glass and bright industrial colour.

Historical background. Developing from 1970s Britain, it turned the building 'inside out', displaying ducts, trusses and lifts as ornament, later evolving toward sleek sustainable 'eco-tech' towers.

Origin. United Kingdom, 1970s; international by the 1990s.

Timeline

1977

Centre Pompidou opens

1986

Lloyd's of London & HSBC

2004

30 St Mary Axe (Gherkin)

2014

Eco-tech towers mainstream

Key Architects

Richard RogersNorman FosterRenzo PianoNicholas Grimshaw

Influential Projects

Centre Pompidou

Piano & Rogers · 1977
Paris, France

Lloyd's Building

Richard Rogers · 1986
London, UK

30 St Mary Axe

Norman Foster · 2004
London, UK

02 — Design Philosophy

Design Philosophy

Core Principles

Structural expressionServices as ornamentFlexibility & modularityPrefabricationHonesty of technology

The Thinking

By externalising structure and services, the interior is freed for flexible use and the façade becomes a legible diagram of exactly how the building works.

Values It Represents

InnovationAdaptabilityTransparencyOptimism about technology

Why It Emerged

Faith in engineering and industrial production to deliver lighter, more flexible, future-proof buildings — architecture as a precise serviceable machine.

03 — Visual Characteristics

Visual Characteristics

MassingLegible volumes with structure and service towers expressed externally; taut and light.
Form LanguageRectilinear or sleekly aerodynamic; later smooth curved forms.
CompositionOrdered by structural module; external masts and bracing.
ProportionSlender, engineered, sized precisely to load.
GeometryTriangulated trusses, cables, repeated bays.
RhythmCrisp mechanical repetition of structure.
SymmetryOften symmetrical and modular.
ScalePavilion to supertall — a precise kit of parts.
Roof TypesExposed trusses, tension structures and glazed atria.
Window ProportionLarge flush glazing between expressed structure.
EntranceGlazed and transparent, with dramatic external lifts/escalators.
Façade ArticulationGlass curtain wall with expressed steel, external bracing, ducts and bright service runs.

04 — Materials

Materials

Structural steel

Slender, strong structure that frees and frames the façade.

Glass curtain wall

Admits light and dissolves the wall between inside and out.

Aluminium panel

Lightweight, precise, factory-finished cladding.

Tension cables

Tension cables that express the flow of structural forces.

Coloured ducts

Externalised services, colour-coded, celebrated as ornament.

05 — Color Palette

Color Palette

Precise, energetic and optimistic — primary colours code function on a metallic field.

Primary

Silver Grey
#aeb3b6
Glass Blue
#a6c0c8

Accent

Pompidou Blue
#2f6fae
Service Red
#c0392b
Duct Yellow
#e3b21d

Material Colors

Steel
#7c8186
Aluminium
#bcc1c4

Typical Combinations

  • +Silver + glass + colour-coded services
  • +Monochrome steel with one bold accent

06 — Façade Design Guide

Façade Design Guide

Window CompositionFull-height flush glazing spanning between expressed structural bays.
EmphasisExpressed structure drives a strong, clear grid.
Shading DevicesExternal louvres, brise-soleil and deep structure for engineered solar control.
BalconiesMaintenance gantries and walkways expressed as structure.
FramesSlim, precise, part of the curtain-wall system.
OpeningsLarge, repetitive, modular; vision glass maximised.
Architectural DetailsCast-steel nodes, bolts and cable terminations celebrated.
OrnamentationNone applied — exposed structure, services and connections are the ornament.

07 — Lighting

Lighting

Natural Lighting Strategy

Daylight floods through curtain walls and atria; external structure modulates glare.

Artificial Lighting Concept

Reveal the skeleton with grazing and accent light; functional task light within.

Night Façade Appearance

The transparent envelope glows, exposing the lit skeleton as a working machine.

Recommendations

Light the structure to celebrate it; colour-accent services; keep glazing transparent at night.

08 — Typical Building Types

Typical Building Types

Where this style performs best:

Office towersCultural centresAirports & terminalsExhibition hallsStadiaFactories

09 — Design Checklist

Design Checklist

Tap an item to mark it complete — saved to your browser.

  • Express the primary structure externally
  • Celebrate or externalise services
  • Use a clear repetitive module
  • Specify steel, glass and aluminium honestly
  • Detail nodes, bolts and cables as craft
  • Maximise transparent flush glazing
  • Add engineered shading (louvres, brise-soleil)
  • Colour-code services for legibility

10 — Common Mistakes

Common Mistakes

Elements that contradict the style — what to avoid:

  • ✕Faking exposed structure with decorative steel
  • ✕Hiding the structure and services the style celebrates
  • ✕Heavy masonry-like massing contradicting lightness
  • ✕Arbitrary structural rhythm
  • ✕Neglecting weathering of exposed parts

11 — AI Prompt Examples

AI Prompt Examples

Optimised for Midjourney, ChatGPT image tools or Claude — exterior renders. Tap copy to use.

Exterior

High-tech building exterior, exposed external steel structure, glass curtain wall, colour-coded service ducts, external lift tubes, transparent, precise engineering, architectural photography --ar 3:2 --style raw

Façade Concept

High-tech façade, expressed steel trusses and tension cables, full-height flush glazing, cast-steel nodes, modular bays, frontal elevation, crisp detail

Night View

High-tech tower exterior at night, glowing transparent envelope revealing lit steel skeleton, illuminated service runs, sharp reflections, architectural night photography

Aerial View

Aerial of a high-tech terminal, vast exposed-truss roof, glazed concourse, modular grid, drone photography, daylight

Street Perspective

Street view of a high-tech building exterior, external bright-coloured ducts and glass lifts, plaza, energetic, documentary photography

Masterplan

High-tech masterplan, modular structural grid, flexible pavilions, expressed circulation spines, axonometric, silver-and-primary palette

Parametric

GlobalExperimental

Modern
